key_insight: The paper gives wavenumber-explicit stability and preasymptotic error bounds for UPML-truncated FEM, showing the method remains reliable for high-frequency Helmholtz scattering.
engineering_relevance: This provides a theoretically justified way to simulate obstacle scattering with fewer truncation artifacts and better high-wavenumber robustness in computational wave mechanics.
method: Derive explicit stability for the truncated UPML problem using stretched Green-kernel estimates.
Prove the inf-sup constant scales like O(k^-1) in a k-weighted H1 norm and obtain exponential PML truncation decay.
Build a linear CIP-FEM on the truncated UPML domain and use piecewise H1+s regularity to get preasymptotic error estimates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical stability/error analysis together with numerical experiments that confirm the predictions in the high-frequency regime.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on handling coefficient jumps and corner singularities at PML interfaces, so implementation details and mesh design remain important.
paper_title: Wavenumber-explicit stability and preasymptotic error analysis of UPML finite element method for obstacle scattering problems
